articles.title: Fast to Deny, Slow to Grant: The Range of Motion En Banc Petition at 102 Days
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇撰写

《全席再审申请》在“活动范围产品公司诉阿玛依德公司案”（联邦巡回上诉法院2026年第166卷第四期第981页）中已悬置102天。活动范围产品公司于2026年4月3日提交申请，此前由坎宁安法官主笔、休斯法官附议的合议庭（摩尔首席法官 dissent）维持了关于不侵权的简易判决。我曾就该案写过三篇分析：合议庭裁决、全席再审申请，以及后续审视同一法律原则在另一待审上诉案中的浮现。

这份全席再审申请提出两个问题，均源自摩尔首席法官的反对意见：

**为何102天至关重要**。联邦巡回法院处理普通全席再审申请通常迅速而低调，多在一个月内以单行 deny 令结案。悬置时间较长的申请，往往意味着至少一位法官要求暂缓、需要启动投票程序，或已催生详细书面意见。因此，历时长短可粗略反映法院对申请的重视程度——当前申请已越过重要时间节点。而每多一天延迟，获得再审的几率便随之增加。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

The petition for rehearing en banc in Range of Motion Products, LLC v. Armaid Co., 166 F.4th 981 (Fed. Cir. 2026), has now been pending for 102 days. Range of Motion filed on April 3, 2026, after a divided panel (Cunningham, J., joined by Hughes, J., with Chief Judge Moore dissenting) affirmed summary judgment of non-infringement.  I have previously written about this case: the panel decision, the en banc petition, and a later look at the same doctrine as it surfaced in a second pending appeal.

The en banc petition raises two questions, both drawn from Chief Judge Moore's dissent:

  • Whether the "plainly dissimilar" threshold from Egyptian Goddess, Inc. v. Swisa, Inc., 543 F.3d 665 (Fed. Cir. 2008) (en banc), inverts the ordinary observer test of Gorham Mfg. Co. v. White, 81 U.S. 511 (1871); and
  • Whether a judge's functionality findings at claim construction improperly take a question from the jury in tension with the Seventh Amendment and Markman v. Westview Instruments, Inc., 517 U.S. 370 (1996).

Why 102 days matters. The Federal Circuit disposes of the ordinary en banc petition quickly and quietly, usually with a one-line order denying rehearing, and usually within about 30 days.

The petitions that sit longer are ones that at least one judge has asked to hold, the ones drawing a request for a vote, or the ones that generate a detailed writing. Elapsed time is therefore a rough signal of how seriously the court is treating a petition, and this one has now passed a meaningful mark.  And, each additional day of delay increases the odds of a rehearing.

articles.title: Still a Lawyer, Not a Practitioner: Schindler v. Squires and the Reach of Practice Before the Office
articles.content:
作者：Dennis Crouch

2026年7月10日，恰逢美国专利商标局执业与纪律办公室提交纪律处分投诉七周年，联邦巡回法院由Reyna、Stoll和Hughes三位法官组成的合议庭就Schindler v. Squires案（案号25-1557）进行了口头辩论。上诉人Edwin D. Schindler自1984年起即为注册专利律师，目前正对一项为期两年的停业处分提出挑战。美国专利商标局认定，Schindler帮助Michael Kroll——这位在多年纪律问题后被取消PTO执业资格的纽约律师——维持其专利申请业务的运营，Kroll将专利工作交给Schindler审阅和提交。主持审理的Reyna法官以一则程序性消息开场：在Schindler就实体问题发表任何意见之前，法院即席宣布驳回了Schindler根据第38条规则提出的针对政府辩护上诉的制裁申请。您可以在SCOTUSGate收听完整辩论录音。

本案有两个特点值得预先提及。首先，Schindler自行代理了本案。“自己代理的律师”这句老话通常是用作玩笑的；但当赌注涉及律师自身的职业纪律，而政府简报又指出对无代理律师当事人的通常体恤并不适用于持有执照的律师时，这话的分量就不同了。其次，政府的代理方并非处理局长从专利审判委员会上诉案件的USPTO Solicitor办公室，而是弗吉尼亚州东区助理检察官Dennis Barghaan。该人员配置源于《美国法典》第35卷第32条，该条款将通过PTO纪律案件的上诉审查导向亚历山大市的地区法院而非直接提交联邦巡回法院：申诉状需送达美国检察官。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

On July 10, 2026, seven years to the day after the USPTO's Office of Enrollment and Discipline filed its disciplinary complaint, a Federal Circuit panel of Judges Reyna, Stoll, and Hughes heard oral argument in Schindler v. Squires, No. 25-1557. The appellant, Edwin D. Schindler, has been a registered patent attorney since 1984 and is challenging a two-year suspension. The USPTO concluded that Schindler helped Michael Kroll, a New York attorney who had been excluded from PTO practice after years of disciplinary trouble, keep his patent prosecution business running, with Kroll sending the patent work to Schindler for review and filing.  Judge Reyna, presiding, opened with a piece of procedural news: before Schindler said a word on the merits, the court announced from the bench that it had denied Schindler's Rule 38 motion seeking sanctions against the government for defending the appeal. You can listen to the full argument at SCOTUSGate.

Two features of the case deserve mention up front. First, Schindler argued his own case. The old saying about the lawyer who represents himself is usually invoked in jest; it lands differently when the stakes are the lawyer's own professional discipline, and the government's brief noted that the usual solicitude for pro se litigants does not extend to licensed attorneys.

Second, the government was represented not by the USPTO Solicitor's Office, which handles the Director's appeals from the PTAB, but by Dennis Barghaan, Assistant U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia. That staffing follows from 35 U.S.C. § 32, which routes review of PTO discipline cases through the district court in Alexandria rather than directly to the Federal Circuit: the petition is served on the U.S. Attorney.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
